page: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory density of 14.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the E96-48as_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Its memory subsystem is particularly robust, achieving a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score, although raw memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached large blocks fall within the average range. Single-core performance is also solid, with stress-ng single-core results placing in the top 25%.

The server's hardware profile makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing tasks like bzip3 decompression, where it performs in the top 25%. However, it is less optimal for large-scale LLM text generation, as evidenced by its poor performance on 70B parameter models.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 3.525 GHz. It features 672.0 GB of RAM, translating to 14.0 GB per core, and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and it lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks highlight top-tier memory performance alongside strong multi-core CPU capabilities, particularly in multi-threaded decompression and encryption tasks. Conversely, memory bandwidth and database operations perform at average levels. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong computational foundation, this server is best suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, in-memory databases, and large-scale data processing applications that do not require local disk storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory performance for database and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 672 GB of RAM for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier PassMark memory benchmark scores
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Strong multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression capabilities
Optimized for memory-intensive enterprise applications and in-memory databases
